data.title: Precious Metals Futures Experience Significant Decline
data.description: According to PANews, precious metals futures have seen a notable decrease. The main contract for Shanghai Silver has dropped over 4%, currently priced at 11,927 yuan per kilogram. Meanwhile, the main contract for Shanghai Gold has fallen nearly 4%, with a current price of 922.86 yuan per gram.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs Experience Significant Outflows
data.description: According to PANews, recent monitoring by Lookonchain reveals that ten Bitcoin ETFs have collectively seen a net outflow of 4,828 BTC, valued at approximately $460 million. Among these, BlackRock reported an outflow of 2,610 BTC, reducing its holdings to 794,948 BTC. Additionally, nine Ethereum ETFs experienced a net outflow of 70,248 ETH, worth around $221 million, with BlackRock accounting for 43,240 ETH of this outflow, bringing its holdings down to 3,828,719 ETH.

data.title: Bybit launches an LBTC on-chain earning event, where users can participate to share a prize pool of 100,000 USDT.
data.description: To celebrate the launch of LBTC on the Bybit on-chain earning platform, the platform is launching a month-long event. Starting today, Bybit users can earn extra annualized rewards by staking BTC to mint LBTC and participate in a prize pool of 100,000 USDT (issued in BARD). Event period: November 14, 2025, 10:00 (UTC) – December 13, 2025, 23:59 (UTC). About LBTC: LBTC is issued by Lombard Finance and backed by the Babylon Labs protocol. It is pegged 1:1 to real BTC and currently holds approximately 60% of the market share. Bybit users can complete staking, minting, and redemption operations in one stop, without the need for additional wallets or cross-chain processes. With LBTC, BTC holders can obtain on-chain staking rewards while fully releasing asset liquidity.
